questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.hint: Think of 15 doubled, then doubled again.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: 36 ÷ 6 = 6. Add the trailing zero to get 60.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: A store has 5 boxes of colored pencils. If each box contains 12 pencils, how many pencils are there in total?
questions.hint: Multiply the number of boxes by the number of pencils per box.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Leo has $30. He buys 2 toy cars that cost $8 each. How much money does he have left?
questions.hint: Find the total cost of the cars first, then subtract from $30.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.hint: Add the numerators together and keep the denominator the same.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Subtract 9/10 - 7/10. Write your answer as a fraction (e.g., a/b).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: What do you call a geometric shape that has 4 equal sides but no right angles?
questions.hint: It looks like a slanted square.
questions.explanation: A rhombus has four equal sides, but unlike a square, its corners are not 90 degrees.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: A square playground has an area of 36 square meters. What is its perimeter in meters?
questions.hint: Find the side length first by figuring out what number multiplied by itself equals 36.
questions.explanation: Since 6 × 6 = 36, each side length is 6 meters. Perimeter = 4 × 6 = 24 meters.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: How many hours are there in half a day?
